t_ch: 所以它被落在囚室附近，应该是另有原因。
t_en: Hence, I believe the book is near the prison for other reasons.

t_ch: 会不会是狱卒留下的？
t_en: Perhaps it was left behind by a prison guard?

t_ch: 可能是哪个粗心的狱卒落下的，但直到今天也没有任何人申报过这样的失物登记。
t_en: Maybe it was left behind by a careless guard, and no one has reported it as lost until today.

t_ch: 会不会是犯人留下的？
t_en: Perhaps it was left behind by a prisoner?

t_ch: 犯人的收押和转移有严格的检查流程，其随身携带的物品都会登记在册，所以不会有这种可能。
t_en: All prisoners go through a rigorous checking process for their admission and transfer, and their belongings are registered, so this is not possible.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您还想到了其他的可能性吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, have you thought of other possibilities?

t_ch: 这世间的偶然与可能性太多了…
t_en: There are numerous possibilities in this world...

t_ch: 没错，常有人说命运的可能性像网，但是回头看它却是线——要是这本书能开口说话，也许它能告诉我们这条线究竟是什么…可惜，它不能。
t_en: Exactly. It's frequently remarked that destiny's possibilities resemble a complex web, yet upon closer inspection, it seems more akin to a single thread — if only this book can speak, maybe it could unveil the secret of that thread. Alas, it can't.

t_ch: 现在知道了也只是满足好奇心。
t_en: Even if we discovered the secret, it would only satisfy our curiosity now.

t_ch: 您不正是为了满足好奇心才来找我的吗？
t_en: Didn't you look for me to satisfy your curiosity?

t_ch: 确实，我无法给{NICKNAME}大人一个满意的解释。幽囚狱内普通犯人们享有被限制的自由，就算说这本书是犯人在狱中书写，也并非是没有可能的……
t_en: Indeed, I'm unable to provide {NICKNAME} with a satisfactory answer. Even within the confines of the Shackling Prison, ordinary inmates maintain a degree of constrained autonomy. Hence, it's not impossible that this book was authored by an inmate within the prison walls...

t_ch: 幽囚狱经常会发生一些奇怪的事，作为司狱我见过太多的事情没头没尾，但让我彻底睁一只眼闭一只眼也说服不了自己……
t_en: Strange things often happen in the Shackling Prison, and as a Jailer, I have seen too many unexplainable things. Even if I were to turn a blind eye, I couldn't convince myself...

t_ch: 于是乎，我会推荐您「简单性原理」——当你的脑中有多个竞争地位的推论时，简单的那个更好…也许它不一定就是真相本身，但是它可能是最有说服力的推论。
t_en: As such, I recommend adopting the "principle of parsimony" — when you have multiple inferences competing in your mind, go for the simplest one... Maybe it's not necessarily the truth itself, but it may be the most compelling inference.

t_ch: 那么{NICKNAME}大人，闭上眼睛，专注于你的思考之上。
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, close your eyes and focus on what's on your mind.

t_ch: …在你初登仙舟之时，你的智库书架就不断提示你各种尚未获得到的书目…无论是有心之人，亦或是无心之人，谁都找不到这本《魔阴身考》。
t_en: ...When you first boarded the Xianzhou, your data bank had persistently notified you of numerous titles yet to be acquired... Despite the varied motivations of those who sought the book, whether deliberate or inadvertent, none succeeded in locating A Study into the Mara-Struck.

t_ch: …而幽囚狱是用来关闭重犯的地方，你曾在这里见过一些*并非是人*的囚徒…那么一本书被关在这里，似乎也算是有理可依。
t_en: ...Moreover, The Shackling Prison is a place for dangerous offenders. You witnessed some non-human criminals in this prison... so it seems plausible that a book would be locked up here.

t_ch: 赤衣大人推荐了你「简单性原理」，希望你删繁就简得到更简洁的结论，那么——
t_en: Chiyi recommends the "principle of parsimony," hoping you'll cut through the clutter and find a more concise conclusion. In that case...

t_ch: 就是这本书被抓起来了。
t_en: This book must have been arrested.

t_ch: 虽然逻辑稍显天马行空，但它好在足够简洁。书犯了错，被抓起来了，它本该早早出现，命运却和它开起了玩笑，它被抓进了你一开始无法前往的幽囚狱……
t_en: While this reasoning may seem fantastical, it holds a succinct truth. The book committed a crime and was captured. It should have appeared sooner, yet fate plays a cruel joke on it. It was confined in The Shackling Prison, a place you couldn't access at first...

t_ch: 应该是某个犯人留下的。
t_en: This book was left behind by a prisoner.

t_ch: 囚室是关犯人的，书又不是犯人，那么就是犯人把书留在囚室附近的。至于书是怎么到犯人手上的…那只是无限将简单的问题扩大罢了。
t_en: The cell is for prisoners. Books aren't prisoners, so the book must have been left behind by a prisoner near the cell. As for how the book came into the possession of the prisoner... that'll further complicate a simple question.

t_ch: 应该是策划忘记投放了。
t_en: The designers forgot to put it in that game.
Shh!

t_ch: 你是在说他们在正式上线的版本中留下了错误，却未在后面的版本开发中及时修复，等到了现在，却又在用「书被抓起来了」的理由搪塞过去？
t_en: Are you suggesting they left it out of the version that officially went live, failed to fix it in time in later versions, and then waited until now to pass it off with an excuse that "the book got arrested"?

t_ch: 好吧，由「简单性原理」来看，这不无可能！他们为此还准备了一点简单的小补偿，收下吧，希望你能心领神会闭口不提。
t_en: All right, based on the "principle of parsimony," this is not impossible! As such, they have prepared a small gift for you as compensation. Please accept it and never mention this incident again.

t_ch: 看来您已经得到了一个足够简洁的答案，我知道您为此事跑前跑后并不容易，我会作为司狱给你一些跑腿的辛苦费用，对您的求知精神表示赞扬！辛苦了！
t_en: It appears you've obtained a sufficiently straightforward answer. I understand the effort involved in pursuing this matter. As a jailer, I will reward you for your effort and dedication to knowledge! Thank you for your hard work!

t_ch: 自从幽囚狱大乱以后，十王司第一时间积极响应，已经将大部分出逃的犯人抓捕入狱，最大程度保留维持着幽囚狱内应有的秩序。
t_en: Ever since The Shackling Prison riot, the Ten-Lords Commission responded in force at first notice — most of the escaped prisoners have already been captured and returned to their cells, with the most stringent measures of security maintained within The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 然而，百密终有一疏。就算冥差、狱卒、幽府武弁统统放弃了休假，日夜不停地加班修复各类出逃问题，仍有一些狡猾的犯人不知踪影。
t_en: Then again, nothing is ever airtight. Even if the spiritfarers, prison guards, and Wraith Warden forgo their vacation days and work overtime to plug escapee problems, there will still be certain cunning convicts who slip away among the shadows.

t_ch: 下面似乎还有很多游荡的怪物…
t_en: It still looks like there's plenty of Wandering Monsters down there...

t_ch: 游荡的怪物？
t_en: Wandering Monsters?

t_ch: 哦——！不打紧，那些怪物想必是前来劫狱的残党，幽囚狱里原本就没有他们的房间…你要是撞上了，只管出手消灭便是，不用向十王司打招呼。
t_en: Ohhh! Don't mind them, those monsters are probably here to plunder the stragglers. The Shackling Prison didn't have space for them to begin with... If you run into one, feel free to take it out, no need to report it to the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 理解，这也是没有办法的事嘛。
t_en: Understood. We can't help it after all.

t_ch: 感谢你的理解。即便十王司面对意外已经做出了充足的准备，然而人们总会因为「一次令人印象深刻的混乱」而对幽囚狱此前种种努力视若不见。
t_en: Thank you for understanding. Even if the Ten-Lords Commission has made the utmost preparations for unforeseen circumstances, The Shackling Prison's various efforts will still be overshadowed by a singular, deeply impressionable instance of chaos in people's eyes.

t_ch: 能得到你的理解，也是一种荣幸。
t_en: It's also considered an honor to gain your understanding.

t_ch: 你回过神来，发现判官雪衣与此地的司狱赤衣正在商谈要事……
t_en: Just as you regain your senses, you notice Judge Xueyi discussing an important matter with the Jailer of this part, Chiyi...

t_ch: 雪衣大人，目前的收押工作进展顺利。不过有五名囚犯目前仍然下落不明……
t_en: Madam Xueyi, our current detainment task is proceeding smoothly. But there are still five convicts whose whereabouts are unknown...

t_ch: 具体来说？
t_en: Please elaborate.

t_ch: 根据监控显示，他们并没有在骚动发生后离开幽囚狱，如今更是没有机会离开。按道理来说他们应该藏在幽囚狱某处，只是我们还没有找到……
t_en: According to surveillance, they did not leave The Shackling Prison after the disturbance, and chances of succeeding will be even slimmer now. Practically speaking, they should be hiding somewhere in The Shackling Prison, it's just that we haven't found them...

t_ch: 犯人的危险程度呢？
t_en: What's the danger level for the prisoners?

t_ch: 极低。
t_en: Extremely low.

t_ch: 危险程度，极低。寻找难度，不明。继续这样找下去，只是在浪费幽囚狱人力。
t_en: Danger level, extremely low. Search difficulty, unknown. We'll just exhaust the prison's manpower if we keep hunting for them like this.

t_ch: 你注意到雪衣与赤衣看向了你，一瞬间你好像明白了很多事，但是你也可以不明白。
t_en: You notice Xueyi and Chiyi looking over at you, and you realize many things in an instant, though you can also pretend to be unaware of them all.

t_ch: 我脸上有东西吗？
t_en: Something on my face?

t_ch: 没有东西。
t_en: It's nothing.

t_ch: 吾等在看汝。
t_en: I'm looking at you.

t_ch: 直说吧，你们要我做什么？
t_en: Just spit it out, what do you need me to do?

t_ch: 雪衣大人，{NICKNAME}大人被你们麻烦过很多次吗？
t_en: Madam Xueyi, has {NICKNAME} been imposed upon by you many times?

t_ch: 很多次。
t_en: Many times.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，此事并非是交托于汝的任务，汝不必将其视为负担。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this isn't a task that has been entrusted to you, you do not need to view it as a burden.

t_ch: 这幽囚狱之中潜藏着五名逃犯，或生或死，统统需要查证清楚，不可留下隐患。汝受十王恩赐在幽囚狱内自由行事，如有发现这些逃犯，请及时通报吾等，自有冥差处置。
t_en: There are five fugitives in The Shackling Prison. Dead or alive, their statuses must all be verified, no stone left unturned. I have been bequeathed free rein of The Shackling Prison by the Ten Lords — if these fugitives are discovered, please report their whereabouts to me immediately. My spiritfarers will handle them accordingly.

t_ch: 换句话说，碰碰运气找找就行。
t_en: In other words, we'll just have to try our luck at finding them.

t_ch: 是的，大人有所发现的话最好，没有也没关系。
t_en: Yes, it would be most excellent if you could find them, though it would also be all right if they can't be found.

t_ch: 将逃犯悉数拿回之后，十王司绝不会亏待汝。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission will treat you well if you subdue every single one of the fugitives.

t_ch: 你注意到逃犯看了你一眼，随即他将目光移开，假装自己什么也看不见。但这只不过是在掩耳盗铃罢了，你又不是为了和他成功对上眼神才来到这里的。
t_en: You notice the fugitive look away hastily after glancing at you, pretending that he hasn't seen anything. But that's just how the cat-and-mouse game is played — you haven't come here just to make eyes at him.

t_ch: 喂，你是逃犯吧。
t_en: Hey, you're an escaped prisoner, aren't you?

t_ch: 你好你好，很高兴认识你。
t_en: Hello hello, nice to meet you.

t_ch: 你挡路了。
t_en: You are in my way.

t_ch: 我已经联系狱卒了。
t_en: I've already informed the prison guards.

t_ch: 哎，多事。
t_en: Tsk, meddlesome.

t_ch: 挡路？说得就像你会出来一样。
t_en: In your way? You say it like you're going to come out here.

t_ch: *沉重的叹息*
t_en: *heavy sigh*

t_ch: 别动，我要过去逮捕你了。
t_en: Don't move, I'm going to go over there to arrest you.

t_ch: 哼，再走近一些也无妨。
t_en: Hmph, it won't matter even if you come a little closer.

t_ch: …我不认识你。这对你而言，是件好事。
t_en: ...I don't know you. That's a good thing for you.

t_ch: 走吧，「魔阴身」发作的时候，最好别在我身旁。
t_en: Go. When the mara strikes, you don't want to be next to me.

t_ch: 你还敢威胁我？
t_en: You dare threaten me?

t_ch: 魔阴发作时，我就是另一个人了。
t_en: When the mara strikes... I become something else.

t_ch: 我打过的魔阴身数不胜数。
t_en: I've fought against countless mara-struck.

t_ch: 人皆有魔阴，或各有不同，我的情况殊为特别。
t_en: We are all mara-stricken in different ways, but my situation is unusual.

t_ch: 你说话真的很像一个人…
t_en: Your speech reminds me of someone...

t_ch: 啊，我之前囚室旁边押来了一名犯人，他说话好酷好酷的…不对，我不该说这么多话，你别问我了。
t_en: Ah, there was a prisoner who was locked in the cell beside me. He spoke in a really cool way... No wait, I've mentioned too much, stop prodding me.

t_ch: 哎，多事。
t_en: Tsk, meddlesome.

t_ch: 你明白了，这人并不打算与你好好交流，他只是想用「魔阴身」将你吓退。聪明的你拿出手机将逃犯的位置发给了司狱赤衣，你做完了你该做的事。
t_en: You understand that this person does not intend to have a proper conversation with you. He just wants to scare you away with his mara-struck self. You cleverly take out your phone and send the fugitive's coordinates to Jailer Chiyi — you have done what you came here to do.

t_ch: 逃犯知道自己无处可逃之后，看向了幽囚狱的高处，那是被古海之水淹没的特殊洞天构造。他一开始就知道自己即便能离开囚室，也无法从幽囚狱中逃走。
t_en: When the fugitive knows that he has nowhere to run, he looks up at the heights of The Shackling Prison — a unique delve formed from the waters of the ancient sea. From the beginning, he already knew that even if he could escape his cell, there would be no liberation from the walls of The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: …生命，已成惩戒。
t_en: ...Life has become a punishment.

t_ch: 赤衣带着狱卒来到你所在的地方，顺利将逃犯带走。
t_en: Chiyi arrives at your location with the prison guards and uneventfully leads the fugitive away.

t_ch: 感谢你，{NICKNAME}大人。能在这里找到逃犯，多亏了你的细心观察。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. It was all thanks to your sharp eye that we found a runaway here.

t_ch: 你注意到犯人散发着比阴寒狱更加寒冷、肃杀的气息。幸好，你并不需要与她交手，你只需要把她的位置告诉司狱大人就可以了。
t_en: You notice the fugitive emanating an air even colder and more murderous than the Grimfrost Hold itself. Good thing you don't have to fight her — just transmitting her location to the Jailer will do.

t_ch: 喂，你是逃犯吧。
t_en: Hey, you're an escaped prisoner, aren't you?

t_ch: *无奈的叹息*
t_en: *resigned sigh*

t_ch: 你好你好。
t_en: Hey hey.

t_ch: 免去寒暄，说说来意吧？
t_en: Let's forgo the pleasantries — what do you want?

t_ch: 你是逃犯，我能有什么来意？
t_en: You're an escapee, what else could I possibly be here for?

t_ch: *无奈的叹息*
t_en: *resigned sigh*

t_ch: 有人声的地方，分外聒噪。
t_en: Where there are people, there is noise.

t_ch: 现在退下，我便饶你一命。
t_en: Stand down and I'll spare you.

t_ch: 要是我不退下呢？
t_en: What if I'm not backing down?

t_ch: 多添一条亡魂罢了。
t_en: Another soon to be departed.

t_ch: 我只负责摇人，不负责卖命。
t_en: My job is just to locate you, not spill blood for it.

t_ch: 无趣。
t_en: Tedious.

t_ch: 要打便打，哪有那么多废话？
t_en: Let's just cut to the chase and duke it out.

t_ch: 准备受死……
t_en: Prepare to die...

t_ch: 我早已…跨过了自己的…极限，徘徊于…魔阴身的边界。
t_en: I have already surpassed my limits and may soon succumb to the influence of mara...

t_ch: *尖锐地咆哮*无尽形寿便是这般滋味！
t_en: *Gives out a piercing howl* This is how an infinite lifespan feels like!

t_ch: 你、你过来啊！
t_en: H—How about you get over here!

t_ch: 你以为自己说这些话很强吗？
t_en: You think you can fool me with that line?

t_ch: 竟然没有被我虚张声势吓到……
t_en: You weren't intimidated by my bluff at all...

t_ch: 就让我银河球棒侠来会会你。
t_en: Allow me, the Galactic Baseballer, to face off against you.

t_ch: 要上吗……
t_en: Are you gonna do it...

t_ch: 我打银河球棒侠……
t_en: The Galactic Baseballer...?

t_ch: 没等你们开始打起来，高冷的逃犯双腿一软再也站不起来了。
t_en: Just as you're about to engage, the aloof fugitive's knees go so weak she sinks to the ground.

t_ch: 赤衣带着狱卒来到你所在的地方，顺利带走了逃犯。
t_en: Chiyi arrives at your location with the prison guards and uneventfully leads the fugitive away.

t_ch: 感谢你，{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这名囚犯并不简单，经过我们一番调查确认，与她一同逃出的犯人有一名死在半路上，看她的精神状态，想必还没能接受吧……
t_en: This convict was a handful. Our investigations have confirmed that one of the convicts that she escaped with died along the way. From her mental state, I reckon she hasn't quite accepted the outcome...

t_ch: 哎呀，还以为在这里不会被发现呢。
t_en: Oof, I was hoping I wouldn't be found here.

t_ch: 我看你还挺会藏的呀。
t_en: You're pretty good at hiding.

t_ch: 在外行商，多少得会一些。
t_en: A traveling merchant needs a knowledge of puzzles.

t_ch: 我不来，你要躲在这里一辈子？
t_en: Were you planning to stay here forever if I hadn't come?

t_ch: 飘摇世间，又何来落脚之处？
t_en: It's hard for a wanderer to find rest in this world.

t_ch: 在下是路经仙舟的行商，不幸卷入某起事件之中，被收押至此实属不幸。
t_en: This humble self is a traveling merchant passing along a Xianzhou ship. Alas, I've been unfortunately embroiled in a certain incident and ended up being detained here.

t_ch: 我见你既不是冥差，也不是狱卒，与在下往日无冤，近日无仇…不如行在下一个方便，权当此处无事发生，算是你我结交朋友，日后必定盟助。
t_en: I see that you are neither a spiritfarer nor a prison guard. You have no past grievances nor recent enmity with me... How about allowing me a convenience and pretending that nothing happened between us? Let us become friends this day, and I will surely aid you in the future.

t_ch: 我担心你这人笑里藏刀…
t_en: I'm worried that you've got a knife behind that cordiality of yours...

t_ch: 呵呵，您说笑了，我这人不谙刀兵。
t_en: Heh-heh, surely you jest, I am unskilled in combat.

t_ch: 油腔滑调的，我凭什么帮你？
t_en: You're too smoothly glib, why would I help you?

t_ch: 在下行商在外，无依无靠，自然明白您也需要广结善缘。
t_en: I am a traveling merchant with no shoulder to lean on in the outer worlds. It is natural that one also needs to forge amicable relationships.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，在下想离开这深海监牢，只是想为一位老友送行。仙舟人不设棺椁和灵位，就这样继续在监牢中待下去，我只担心出狱后，朋友无处可寻。
t_en: Truth be told, the reason I wish to leave this abyssal prison is simply to send an old friend off. Xianzhou natives bear no coffins or tablets. If I continue to stay on in this prison, I fear that I'll find my friend nowhere when I get out.

t_ch: 在下一介行商，知晓市值盈亏的规律——凡夺取的，必将偿还；已有之事，必将再来。我终将回到自己的囚室之中…只是在此之前，还有很多事可以做罢了。
t_en: As a traveling merchant, I understand the laws of profit and loss — what is taken must be repaid in kind. What has happened will happen again. I will eventually return to my own cell... but before that, I have many matters to attend to.

t_ch: 说明缘由，司狱会帮你的。
t_en: State your reasons and the Jailer will help you.

t_ch: 是在下小看你了。
t_en: I have underestimated you.

t_ch: 我代你去安葬你的朋友…
t_en: I will bury your friend in your stead...

t_ch: …这是在下的使命。
t_en: ...This is my duty to bear.

t_ch: 你说话的方式好像一位熟人。
t_en: The way you speak reminds me of someone familiar.

t_ch: …是吗？行商们大多是这样说话的吧。
t_en: ...Is that so? Many traveling merchants speak this way.

t_ch: 我听见了狱卒的脚步声，看来清算的时间到了。可惜，没来得及说动您。
t_en: I hear the footsteps of the prison guards. It appears the time has come for settlement. A pity that I did not manage to persuade you.

t_ch: 赤衣带着狱卒来到你所在的地方，顺利将逃犯带走。
t_en: Chiyi arrives at your location with the prison guards and uneventfully leads the fugitive away.

t_ch: 感谢你，{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这名囚犯并不简单，根据一番调查确认，他口中的「老友」也是逃狱五人组其中一人。据说那位「老友」的纹身掌握着「黄金乡」的秘密，所谓「归葬」只是逃犯的托辞。
t_en: This prisoner is no simple individual. According to our investigation, the old friend he mentioned is also one of the fugitive quintet. Apparently, that old friend has a tattoo that holds Goldville's secret. This so-called burial is just a code word among them.

t_ch: 我是十王司幽囚狱分区的司狱赤衣，负责将你们收监…你们记得我么？
t_en: I am Chiyi, Jailer of the Ten-Lords Commission's Shackling Prison zone. I have been tasked with taking you all in... Do you remember me?

t_ch: 记得。
t_en: I remember.

t_ch: …不记得了。
t_en: ...Not at all.

t_ch: 呵呵，你看，我们这不就回来了么。
t_en: Heh-heh, look, we all ended up back here anyway.

t_ch: 少说两句，我或许会赏你个痛快。
t_en: Less prattle from you, and I might just make it quick for you.

t_ch: 人有五名，回来的只有三个……
t_en: There are five, but only three have returned...

t_ch: 嘁。
t_en: Shh.

t_ch: 在逃五名犯人，在狱中相识，试图寻找其中一人承诺的「黄金乡」，由于越狱未果，最后五人越狱，三人收监，剩余二人死于谁手，十王司自会查清楚。
t_en: The escaped five met in prison, and attempted to seek this "Goldville" that one of them had promised. Their jailbreak was thwarted, and of the five who escaped, three have been retrieved, while the remaining two have been murdered by an unseen hand. The Ten-Lords Commission will get to the bottom of this.

t_ch: 呵呵，被卷入了麻烦事之中，自己的真心如何并不重要，重要的是如何找到适合自己扮演的角色…哎，可惜我扮演错了角色。
t_en: Heh-heh, when in a difficult situation, it's not so important to maintain who you truly are, but to find the suitable role to play... Alas, I played the wrong character.

t_ch: 闭嘴。
t_en: Silence.

t_ch: *沉重的喘息*
t_en: *heavy gasp*

t_ch: 狱卒听令，将这三人全部收押。
t_en: Prison guards, heed this order and arrest the trio of convicts.

t_ch: 你看见这三人被再次押往幽狱深处，一场小小风波就此结束了。
t_en: You watch on as the three are once again banished to the depths of The Shackling Prison, marking an end to this minor kerfuffle.

t_ch: 感谢你，能够找到这些冥顽不灵的逃犯，全都要多亏了你。
t_en: We thank you for finding these persistent fugitives. You really came through for us.

t_ch: 所以，他们在自相残杀？
t_en: So, they were trying to kill one another?

t_ch: 「自相残杀」？他们连彼此的名字都不认识，只不过是利益一致的囚徒。
t_en: "Trying to kill each other"? They don't even know each other's names. They're just prisoners that share interests.

t_ch: 可是后来，他们每个人所追求的东西与最初并不一样，于是又产生了变故。
t_en: But later, what they each sought was different from how it began, so there was a change in the situation.

t_ch: 这些人，难道说非常强？
t_en: Are these people really strong then?

t_ch: …我倒是看不出来？他们确实擅长用说话方式让你产生一种「他们很厉害」的印象。
t_en: ...I can't tell, though. They are certainly adept at using words to give off the impression that they are very strong.

t_ch: 不过我早已经说过了，犯人危险程度极低。他们与这狱中大部分的怪物相比，还是太过孱弱了，只需狱卒数人便足以镇压。
t_en: But like I said before: The danger level of these fugitives is extremely low. They are nothing compared to the numerous monsters caged in this prison — a dozen guards is all it would take to subdue them.

t_ch: 感谢您的辛勤奔波，这是十王司的答谢，请您务必收下。
t_en: Thank you for all your efforts. This is a token of appreciation from the Ten-Lords Commission, you must accept it.

t_ch: 感觉这附近会有雪衣提到的逃犯，也许可以找找看……
t_en: It feels like the escapee that Xueyi mentioned is in the vicinity, maybe I'll take a look around...

t_ch: 好像听到了有人走动的声音，应该不是狱卒，而是逃犯……
t_en: I think I hear footsteps — it's probably not a guard's, but the fugitive's...

t_ch: 有人藏在这附近…难道说是雪衣提到的逃犯？
t_en: Someone's hiding nearby... Could it be the escapee that Xueyi mentioned?

t_ch: 请、请留步！那边那位{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}！
t_en: P—Please wait a moment! That {F#young lady}{M#young gentleman} over there!

t_ch: 你是在说我？
t_en: Are you talking to me?

t_ch: 你还记得我俩吧，一个智械加上一位皮皮西人，我们俩来自匹诺康尼。
t_en: Do you still remember us? An Intellitron and a Pepeshi from Penacony.

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}肯定记得，我问{F#她}{M#他}有什么比较值得一去的景点，{F#她}{M#他}说是「爬上建木之巅」。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} definitely remembers. When I asked {F#her}{M#him} which tourist spot was worth visiting, {F#she}{M#he} told me to climb to the top of the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 咱们现在这个处境都要怪{F#她}{M#他}？
t_en: So, our current predicament is all thanks to {F#her}{M#him}?

t_ch: 也有一半原因在我们信了{F#她}{M#他}的鬼话吧……
t_en: Well, it's also partly because we believed in {F#her}{M#his} nonsense...

t_ch: 我们俩…就像你看到的，行动不便，能麻烦你过来一下吗？
t_en: As you can see... The two of us can't really move. Can you please come over here for a second?

t_ch: 我好像没听见什么…
t_en: I don't think I hear anything...

t_ch: 喂——是我呀，是我。
t_en: Hey! It's me. It's me!

t_ch: 你清楚记得有一种赛博诈骗的方式就是假装与你认识，嘴里一直说着「是我呀，是我」，心里却等着你说出某个「模糊不清的身份」。
t_en: You clearly remember a cyber scam where the scammer pretends to be someone you know. They repeat the phrase "it's me, it's me," hoping that you'll mention a vague identity that they'll be able to use.

t_ch: 不过你若是有空，随时都可以回来再调查一番，你不会上当的。
t_en: However, you can always come back and investigate once you're free. After all, you'll never fall for it.

t_ch: 你放心，我们并不是来报复你的。一来，这里是监狱，幽府武弁都在盯着我们呢；二来，很明显，我们应该是打不过你的。
t_en: Don't worry, we're not here to take revenge. After all, this is a prison, and the Wraith Wardens are all watching us. Also, we're obviously not a match for you.

t_ch: 说到底还是想打我？
t_en: So, at the end of the day, you still want to get back at me?

t_ch: 就算我们做不到，有这样的心理活动也是人之常情嘛。
t_en: Even though we're not a match for you, it's only natural for us to have such thoughts.

t_ch: 所以你们是真的爬建木去了？
t_en: So, did you really climb the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 你猜猜为什么我们会在幽囚狱见面？
t_en: Why don't you make a guess as to why we're in the Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 你的粉头发伙伴已经警告过我们了，是我们自己没控制住好奇心。
t_en: Even though your pink-haired companion warned us, we could not suppress our curiosity.

t_ch: 我们本来还在丹鼎司四处找人询问如何爬建木，结果那个小孩就是十王司的。
t_en: We were looking around the Alchemy Commission to find someone to ask how we could climb the Ambrosial Arbor. However, we didn't expect the little kid that we asked was from the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟真是危机四伏啊。
t_en: Danger really lurks in every corner of the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 需要我帮你们去解释一下吗？
t_en: Do you need me to explain things for you?

t_ch: 倒是不用。他们似乎确认了我们并非是「求药使」，打算放我们走了。
t_en: Oh, that won't be necessary. It seems like they have verified that we're not Elixir Seekers and have decided to let us go.

t_ch: 结果根本就没有爬上建木吗？
t_en: So, you didn't get to climb the Ambrosial Arbor in the end?

t_ch: 人家建木是有持明族封印的，咱们没有通天的本事，一辈子都接近不了那里……
t_en: We're not omnipotent. The Ambrosial Arbor was sealed by the Vidyadhara. There was no way we could even get close to it.

t_ch: 不过，这次来幽囚狱一趟也算是有所收获。幽囚狱就很适合开发梦境素材！
t_en: However, we can't say that nothing good came from our trip to the Shackling Prison. The Shackling Prison is such a suitable place for developing Dreamscape material!

t_ch: 没错，虽然十王司的人说要把我有关幽囚狱地形结构的记忆删除掉……
t_en: That's right, although the people from the Ten-Lords Commission said that they'll erase the part of my memory related to the layout and structure of the Shackling Prison...

t_ch: 虽然这并非你的本意，但你赋予我们第二次艺术生命，我愿称你为我的艺父。
t_en: Although it wasn't your intention, you have given our artistic careers a new lease of life. I am willing to address you as our artistic parent.

t_ch: 剧本已经想得差不多了。故事讲述一名步离银行家含冤入狱，又在监狱中重振旗鼓步步为营自我拯救，最终逃出幽囚狱重获自由，自证清白。
t_en: The script is almost ready. The story will be about a borisin banker who was wrongfully imprisoned and his journey of self-salvation within the prison. In the end, he regained his freedom by escaping from the prison and managed to prove his own innocence.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼人最喜欢看这种穷小子发家的故事！嘿嘿。
t_en: Penaconians love this sort of rags-to-riches story! Heh heh.

t_ch: 别让十王司的人知道。
t_en: Don't let the Ten-Lords Commission find out.

t_ch: 你提醒的有道理…我刚才差点就去和他们谈「圣地巡礼」的合作了。
t_en: Thanks for your timely reminder... I almost went to discuss with them about organizing a pilgrimage here.

t_ch: 总感觉有一种似曾相识的感觉。
t_en: Why does this plot sound so familiar...

t_ch: 好的剧本都是有艺术共性的，我已经决定叫它「幽囚狱的救赎」了。
t_en: Good scripts often have high degrees of artistic commonality. I have decided to call this film "The Shackling Prison Redemption."

t_ch: 我们还有些手续就能离开这里，接下来的日子，打算老老实实地享受采风之旅了。
t_en: We still have some paperwork to complete before we can leave this place. For the remainder of our time on the Xianzhou, we plan to spend our time searching for inspiration.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 我们还有些手续办完就离开这里了。
t_en: We still have some paperwork to complete before we can leave this place.

t_ch: 以后再也不想爬建木的事了。
t_en: I'll never think about climbing the Ambrosial Arbor ever again.

t_ch: 这不是给我俩指路的好心人吗？真巧，在这里遇见您了。
t_en: Hey, isn't that the kind-hearted soul who showed us the way? What a coincidence, I didn't expect to see you here.

t_ch: 你可能已经认不出我了，别说是你，刚才我自己也不敢相信自己胖了好多！
t_en: Perhaps you don't recognize me, because even I can't believe how much weight I've put on!

t_ch: 说实在话，我看不出来…
t_en: To be honest, I really can't tell the difference...

t_ch: 是吗？我以为很明显了，那我还能吃！
t_en: Really? I thought that it was very obvious. In that case, I probably still can eat more!

t_ch: 你已经吃得很多了。
t_en: You're already eating a lot.

t_ch: 金人巷确实是个好地方吧？
t_en: Aurum Alley is a great place, right?

t_ch: 味道是真的没话说，我们品尝过很多世界的美食，但我的味觉感知系统还是第一次这么愉悦。
t_en: The flavors are exceptional. Despite eating various delicacies from all over the universe, it was the first time my taste perception system felt such elation.

t_ch: 这么好吃的东西，不是在所有地方都能吃到，而且还得现吃现做……
t_en: It's not just anywhere that we get to taste such wonderful delicacies. And it's even freshly prepared...

t_ch: 掌握大厨的厨艺是要时间的。
t_en: It takes a lot of time to master the skills of a master chef.

t_ch: 没错，但是梦境里你可以体验大厨的经历！
t_en: You're right, but you can also undergo the master chef experience in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 只能让大家都到金人巷来了…
t_en: We can only get everyone to come to Aurum Alley...

t_ch: 您忘了我们此行目的正是为梦境取材了吗？
t_en: Did you forget that the true purpose of our trip is to obtain Dreamscape material?

t_ch: 剧本已经想得差不多了。金人巷酷爱美食的谛听一心想成为伟大的厨师，于是趴在咱们主人公的头上，操纵着主人公做出地道的金人巷美食！
t_en: The script is almost ready. There is a Diting who loves food in Aurum Alley, and it dreams of becoming a master chef. Therefore, it climbs onto the head of the protagonist and controls the protagonist to make authentic Aurum Alley cuisine!

t_ch: 结果谛听才是厨师，我不是？
t_en: You mean the Diting is the chef and not me?

t_ch: 没了你，谛听也做不了饭。你们俩是一心同体的金人巷大厨。
t_en: Without you, the Diting can't make food either. Together, the two of you make the great chef of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 谛听有些太重了，小老鼠如何？
t_en: A Diting sounds a little too heavy, how about a rat?

t_ch: 我已经决定叫它「小谛听大厨」了，这才算有金人巷特色。
t_en: I've decided to call the film "Diting's Delicacies" to showcase the characteristics of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 我们还有几天就要离开罗浮了，但还有好多美食没吃到，得加把劲了。
t_en: We only have a few days left before we have to leave the Luofu, but there's still so much that we haven't eaten. It seems like we will have to pick up our pace.

t_ch: 要是你的肚子里也有原子炉就没有这些烦恼了。
t_en: If only you have an atomic furnace in your stomach too. Then, we wouldn't have any problems.

t_ch: 嘴巴根本停不下来！
t_en: But I just can't help myself!

t_ch: 你还是停一停吧，不然咱们得去丹鼎司了。
t_en: I think you'd better take a break. Otherwise, we'll be visiting the Alchemy Commission next.

t_ch: 这不是给我俩指路的好心人吗？真巧，在这里遇见您了。
t_en: Hey, isn't that the kind-hearted soul who showed us the way? What a coincidence, I didn't expect to see you here.

t_ch: 我自诩看过不少恐怖片，但在绥园的遭遇还是让我吓了一身冷汗…准确来说，是防止处理器过热的冷却液。
t_en: I pride myself for having watched many horror films, but my encounters at Fyxestroll Garden left me in cold sweat... Or more accurately, caused the coolant that prevents my processing unit from overheating to evaporate.

t_ch: 你们遇到了什么可怕的事？
t_en: What frightening things did you run into?

t_ch: 我时不时能听到很尖锐的女声在唱一种不太理解的戏曲。结果周围根本没有任何音频装置，按理来说声音是通过振动发生的，所以那是什么……
t_en: I occasionally heard a sharp female voice singing the tunes of an indecipherable opera, but I could not find any audio device nearby. Technically speaking, sound is produced by vibration, so I'm not sure what I experienced...

t_ch: 我说那是鬼，但是铁皮脑袋不相信！
t_en: I said that it was a ghost, but this metalhead didn't believe me!

t_ch: 我绝不相信！
t_en: I definitely do not believe that!

t_ch: 其实都没有什么真正的危害。
t_en: Actually, you were not in any real danger.

t_ch: 我感觉比那些血浆横飞的恐怖作品更吓人，是一种心理恐怖，不要问我一个智械为什么知道心理恐怖，我就是知道。
t_en: I think that such psychological fear is much more frightening than the goriest and most gruesome film. Don't ask how an Intellitron knows what psychological fear is, I just do.

t_ch: 不过，这次来绥园一趟也算收获了不少创作灵感，这些素材用来做噩梦也不错！放心，我们会尽量让这样的体验控制在适度娱乐的程度……
t_en: But I've also gained quite a bit of inspiration during this trip to the Fyxestroll Garden. These materials will be ideal for a Nightmare! Don't worry, we'll do our best to ensure that the experience remains suitably enjoyable still...

t_ch: 剧本还没有想好，可能是因为我还没有办法好好理解「鬼魂」是什么…对了，我想问你，你相信鬼魂的存在吗？
t_en: Perhaps it's because I don't fully understand what a ghost is that I haven't managed to come up with the script... That's right, do you believe in the existence of ghosts?

t_ch: 我相信，很多事情都无法解释。
t_en: I believe that they exist. After all, many things in life cannot be explained.

t_ch: 没法解释，就要怪鬼魂了吗？
t_en: Are ghosts really to be blamed just because some things cannot be explained?

t_ch: 没错，我劝你还是早点走出自己的创作舒适区！
t_en: Of course. So, I advise you to just come out of your creative comfort zone.

t_ch: 我不信，仙舟的鬼魂都是岁阳！
t_en: I don't believe that they exist. The ghosts on Xianzhou are perhaps all heliobi!

t_ch: 岁阳的话，我就能理解了，谢谢你，我有些灵感了！
t_en: If they're just heliobi, then I can accept it. Thank you. I can see the ideas forming in my head!

t_ch: 我不管你灵感是什么，梦境游客想看的还是鬼魂啊！
t_en: I don't care what ideas you're forming. What the guests of the Dreamscape want to see are actual ghosts!

t_ch: 十王司的人好像对我们在这里采集素材有些不满。
t_en: It seems like the people from the Ten-Lords Commission are unhappy with us gathering material here.

t_ch: 我们采集得差不多了之后就早点走吧！
t_en: Let's hurry up and leave once we're more or less done!

t_ch: 我感觉有东西一直跟着我们。
t_en: I feel like something's following us.

t_ch: 你…你别吓我。
t_en: D—Don't scare me like that.

t_ch: 这不是给我俩指路的好心人吗？真巧，在这里遇见您了。
t_en: Hey, isn't that the kind-hearted soul who showed us the way? What a coincidence, I didn't expect to see you here.

t_ch: 我们俩之前也是去过不少世界的海边了，鳞渊境总带给我一种特别的感觉。就像是在这里隐藏着某种巨大的力量。
t_en: Even though both of us have visited the seas of numerous worlds, the Scalegorge Waterscape still feels special to me. It feels as if there's a great and mysterious power hidden within this area.

t_ch: 本来随便走走，去收集些梦境素材，但是前面好像是持明族圣地，不让去。
t_en: We just wanted to loiter around and obtain some Dreamscape material, but it seems like the area ahead is the holy place of the Vidyadhara and entry is restricted.

t_ch: 所以，我在想这里其实也没有别人，我们是不是可以……
t_en: But since there's no one else in the area, perhaps we can...

t_ch: 我建议你还是不要去了。
t_en: I would suggest that you don't go there.

t_ch: 很合理的建议。据我推测，我们出发来这里之前，持明族和十王司已经派人来盯着我们了，这里虽然没人，但不代表着他们不知道。
t_en: A reasonable suggestion. According to my deduction, the Vidyadhara and the Ten-Lords Commission have already sent people to watch us even before we set off for this place. Even though there's no one around, it doesn't mean that they won't find out.

t_ch: 有道理，我们皮皮西人向来尊重其他族人，不惹麻烦。
t_en: You have a point. We Pepeshi always respect the people of other races and don't cause trouble for them.

t_ch: 你们去就去，千万别提我。
t_en: Just don't mention that you've met me if you're planning to go there.

t_ch: 你这样急于与我们撇清关系，我明白了，要是继续深入会惹上麻烦。
t_en: Judging by your eagerness to stay clear from us, I've figured out that we'll get ourselves in trouble if we proceed further.

t_ch: 呵呵，我们皮皮西人向来拥有生存的智慧，我们不会进去的。
t_en: Heh, survival instinct is something common among all Pepeshi, so we will not enter the area.

t_ch: 看到这里的波月古海潮起潮落，我感觉到一种内心的宁静，也许我们不需要剧情。
t_en: As I look at the ebb and flow of the Lunarescent Depths, I feel a sense of tranquility. Perhaps, we don't really need a plot.

t_ch: 剧本不用写了，剪辑也不需要，主打一个原汁原味的沉浸式体验。
t_en: Forget the script and the editing. Let's focus on an original and authentic immersive experience.

t_ch: 真的会有人需要这样的梦境吗？

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的有钱人不缺钱，缺的是独一无二的体验。咱们提供的体验足够独特，就自然会有人需要。
t_en: In Penacony, the rich do not lack money. All they seek is a unique experience. If the experience we're providing is unique enough, naturally there will be demand.

t_ch: 你们这么干活，换我也能做啊。
t_en: Seeing the way the two of you work, I reckon I can do it as well.

t_ch: 诶，您这就不懂了。有时候「少就是多」，尤其是在匹诺康尼这样处处被填满的地方，鳞渊境的空寂会更受欢迎。
t_en: *sigh* You don't understand. Sometimes, less is more. Especially in a place like Penacony where everything is already so crammed, the serene and empty Scalegorge Waterscape will be even more popular.

t_ch: 我好像听到了什么巨大生物的声音，这段很震撼啊！
t_en: I think I hear sounds made by a massive creature. This part is pretty shocking!

t_ch: 先做个自我介绍吧，你可以称呼我为「白鸟」。

t_ch: 犯罪行为？我现在就去找狱卒！
t_en: Are you breaking the law? I'll go get a prison guard right now!

t_ch: 等等！别急嘛，你有点太「守法公民」了！你听我解释。我是阿合马铁窗集团的首席物流官，我的本体还在坐牢，别担心！
t_en: Hold on! Calm down. You don't have to be such a "law-abiding citizen"! Let me explain. I'm the Chief Logistics Officer of the Arma Correctional Group and my actual body is currently locked up in jail. You don't have to worry!

t_ch: 先做个自我介绍吧，你可以称呼我为「白鸟」。
t_en: First, let me introduce myself. You can call me Shiratori.

t_ch: 我是不是在哪里见过你，白鸟？
t_en: Shiratori? Have we met somewhere?

t_ch: 原来是恩人啊，我就说还有谁会在幽囚狱里闲逛呢！
t_en: Oh, I recognize you now! Thanks for helping me out the other time. Well, it all makes sense now. Nobody else would be sauntering around the Shackling Prison so casually.

t_ch: 听好了，我知道你有很多想问的。你先问吧，问完以后，我…我需要你的帮助。
t_en: You must have a lot of questions. Ask me whatever you want, but listen, when you're done, I... I hope you can lend me your assistance.

t_ch: 这里具体是干什么的地方？

t_ch: 你目所能及之处都是集装箱对吧？这是幽囚狱存放证物的仓库。
t_en: You see all these freight containers around us? This warehouse is where the Shackling Prison stores all the articles that were used as evidence in their cases.

t_ch: 我听说前段时间幽囚狱暴动，这里是重灾区，有被收押的证物活了过来，也不知道是真的还是假的……
t_en: I heard that this place was badly hit by the riot that broke out in the Shackling Prison a while ago. There are even stories about evidence in custody coming back to life, though nobody knows how much truth there is to those claims...

t_ch: 不过这里是幽囚狱，倒也不奇怪。
t_en: Still, given the sort of place the Shackling Prison is, I won't be surprised if they turned out to be true.

t_ch: 这里有什么你想要的东西？
t_en: What are you looking for in here?

t_ch: 这里有丰富的资源，有异化造物，甚至有某些世界旧文明遗产…全是珍贵的财富。也有狱友说，这集装箱里是紊乱的空间、怪物、以及灾难。
t_en: This place has plenty of resources, including mutant creations and even cultural relics from ancient civilizations... You can say everything in here is a treasure. I even heard from other inmates that within these containers lie things like monsters, turbulent spaces, and even calamities.

t_ch: 而我寻找的，只是一个保险柜，就在这证物仓库里。至于保险箱里是什么，您就不必打听了。
t_en: Myself, I'm just looking for a certain safe inside this warehouse. As for its contents... Sorry, but it's private.

t_ch: 为什么非得叫上我一起来？
t_en: Why do you need me to come along with you?

t_ch: 在幽囚狱里坐过牢的朋友应该都知道：「不要进入证物仓库的集装箱，至少，不要『独自』进入集装箱。」
t_en: Any prisoner who's been in the Shackling Prison long enough knows a certain rule: "Never enter the freight containers in the evidence storage warehouse... At least, not alone."

t_ch: 去往那里必须做好完全的准备，结交有实力的伙伴，以及最重要的——找到一位「认路的」，只有他们有能力将从迷宫般的集装箱里引导出来。
t_en: Anyone planning to venture within must ensure that they're as well-prepared as possible and enlist the aid of strong, dependable allies in advance. Most importantly, they must also engage a "pathfinder," someone who can guide them out of those maze-like containers.

t_ch: 这么重要，你就随便找个人？
t_en: You're grabbing just anyone for something so important?

t_ch: 这里没有其他逃犯，我也不可能找狱卒…除了你，似乎找不了其他人。
t_en: There are no other fugitives here, and it's not like I can approach the prison guards for this... You're the only option I have.

t_ch: 所以，你觉得我是「认路的」？
t_en: So, you think I'm a pathfinder?

t_ch: …你可以是，你最好是。
t_en: I think you are... And I hope you are.

t_ch: …我只有你了。
t_en: ...You're the only one who can help me now.

t_ch: 说吧，需要什么帮助？
t_en: Alright. What do you need me to do?

t_ch: 接下来我要从这集装箱里钻进去，去找到一个保险箱，但是这些集装箱空间紊乱，一旦走进去，没人能靠自己在里面找到方向……
t_en: In a while, I'm going to enter this freight container to look for a security safe, but as I mentioned, the space inside these containers is in chaos. Nobody that enters can orient themselves without help...

t_ch: 所以，*老前辈留下的路线指示*是重要的！我只能靠你了，那就是个纸条，估计是被什么多事的冥差给带到勘录舍了，你能帮我取回来吗？
t_en: ...Which is why the directions left behind by past explorers are so important. Please, you're my only hope now. The directions have been written on a piece of paper, but someone — a meddling spiritfarer, I reckon — has taken it to the Scriptorium. Can you retrieve it for me?

t_ch: 冥差真的能让我带过来吗？
t_en: Will the spiritfarers really give me the memo?

t_ch: 那个上面全是「嗯嗯嗯」的密文，冥差他们看不懂的，你去拿回来就行。
t_en: The directions are written in a cipher, so all they'll see is a mess of mm's and hm's. The spiritfarers won't understand a word. All you have to do is retrieve it and bring it back to me.

t_ch: *老前辈留下的路线指示*是重要的！那就是个纸条，被冥差给带到勘录舍了，你能帮我取回来吗？拜托了！
t_en: Those directions left behind by past explorers are extremely important! They have been written down on a piece of paper, but the memo has been taken to the Scriptorium by a spiritfarer. Can you retrieve it for me? Please, I beg of you!

t_ch: 正如你所见，这略微寒酸的纸条正是白鸟想要的。
t_en: From what you can see, this crummy piece of paper is exactly what Shiratori is looking for.

t_ch: 嗯呢呢，呢嗯嗯，嗯呢呢，嗯呢呢？
t_en: "Ehn-nah-nah, Nah-ehn-ehn, Ehn-nah-nah, Ehn-nah-nah"?

t_ch: 十王司的冥差对这张纸条并不上心的原因找到了。
t_en: You now understand why the spiritfarers of the Ten-Lords Commission pay no attention to this memo.

t_ch: 带着它<color=#dbc291ff>去证物仓库</color>附近找找白鸟吧。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Go to the evidence storage warehouse</color> with it and look for Shiratori nearby.

t_ch: 你回来了！怎么样，找到了我想要的纸条了吗？
t_en: You're back! How did it go? Did you find the memo I wanted?

t_ch: （将嗯嗯嗯纸条交给白鸟）
t_en: (Hand the memo covered in letters to Shiratori)

t_ch: 看来应该是没有问题了！就是这个前人留下了密文和密钥。
t_en: Awesome, looks like we're good to go! This memo was written by the explorer who left behind the cipher and encryption key.

t_ch: 顺带一提，我不会解读前人留下的线索，就只有靠你了。
t_en: By the way, I haven't a clue about how to decrypt the hints they left behind. Can I leave that to you?

t_ch: 我试试吧。
t_en: I'll give it a try.

t_ch: 没了我，你可咋办呀。
t_en: What will you do without me?

t_ch: 密文是：嗯呢呢，呢嗯嗯，嗯呢呢，嗯呢呢。
t_en: The code is "Ehn-nah-nah, Nah-ehn-ehn, Ehn-nah-nah, Ehn-nah-nah."

t_ch: 别嗯了！

t_en: Hm? Hmmm?

t_ch: 嗯嗯。
t_en: Mm-hmm.

t_en: Here's the encryption key: "A" corresponds to "Ehn-nah," "B" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-ehn-ehn," "C" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-nah-ehn," "D" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-ehn"... "S" corresponds to "Ehn-ehn-ehn"... "W" corresponds to "Ehn-nah-nah," ...

t_ch: 别嗯了！
t_en: Stop grunting!

t_ch: 嗯？嗯？嗯？
t_en: Hm? Hmmm?

t_ch: 嗯嗯。
t_en: Mm-hmm.

t_ch: 我就「嗯」到这里了，你有头绪了吗？
t_en: And that's about it, mm-hmm. Have you any clue about how to go about this?

t_ch: 这需要「脱密变换」。
t_en: We need to decrypt the cipher.

t_ch: 看来你果然是懂的，就靠你来引导我了！
t_en: I knew you would know what to do! Alright, I'm counting on you to lead the way.

t_ch: 我没有任何头绪……
t_en: I'm totally lost...

t_ch: 咱们总得试试嘛。
t_en: We have to start somewhere.

t_ch: 密文和密钥随时可以查，别担心！那…「认路的」，我出发了？
t_en: Don't worry, you can check the cipher and encryption key again anytime you want. Well, it's time for me to set off, my "pathfinding" friend.

t_ch: 走吧，小心别死在里边。
t_en: Let's get cracking. Take care of yourself in there.

t_ch: 我就靠你的声音了哦，保持联系。
t_en: I'm counting on you to tell me where to go. Stay in touch!

t_ch: 集装箱探索，开始！
t_en: Freight container exploration, let's do this!

t_ch: 等等，我还有些别的事。
t_en: Hold on, I have other things to do.

t_ch: 密文和密钥随时可以查，别担心！那…「认路的」，我出发了？
t_en: Don't worry, you can check the cipher and encryption key again anytime you want. Well, it's time for me to set off, my "pathfinding" friend.

t_ch: 走吧，小心别死在里边。
t_en: Let's get cracking. Take care of yourself in there.

t_ch: 我就靠你的声音了哦，保持联系。
t_en: I'm counting on you to tell me where to go. Stay in touch!

t_ch: 集装箱探索，开始！
t_en: Freight container exploration, let's do this!

t_ch: 等等，我还有些别的事。
t_en: Hold on, I have other things to do.

t_ch: 我已经在集装箱里了，能听见我的声音吧，从这里开始，我该怎么走？
t_en: Can you hear me? I got into the container. Where do I go from here?

t_ch: 我知道怎么走了！
t_en: I've figured out the way!

t_ch: 哦哦，好的！
t_en: Okay!

t_ch: 向左，向上，向上，向右。
t_en: Go left, up, up, and right.

t_ch: 向上，向右，向上，向上。
t_en: Go up, right, up, and up again.

t_en: Go right, right, up, and right.

t_en: Let me think.

t_en: I'll move according to your instructions!

t_en: Can you tell me about the cipher and encryption key again?

t_en: The code is "Ehn-nah-nah, Nah-ehn-ehn, Ehn-nah-nah, Ehn-nah-nah."

t_ch: 密钥是：「A」为嗯呢，「B」为呢嗯嗯嗯，「C」为呢嗯呢嗯，「D」为呢嗯嗯…「S」为嗯嗯嗯…「W」为嗯呢呢……
t_en: The encryption key goes like this: "A" corresponds to "Ehn-nah," "B" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-ehn-ehn," "C" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-nah-ehn," "D" corresponds to "Nah-ehn-ehn"... "S" corresponds to "Ehn-ehn-ehn"... "W" corresponds to "Ehn-nah-nah," ...

t_ch: 这些字母又是什么意思呢？
t_en: What is the significance of these letters?

t_ch: 根据前人所说，这似乎与*键盘*有关系，似乎「W」为「向上」，「S」为「向下」，「D」为「向右」…剩下的我也不明白。
t_en: According to that explorer, there seems to be some kind of connection between the letters and the keyboard. The encryption key suggests that "W" corresponds to "up," "S" to "down," and "D" to "right," but I have no clue beyond that.

t_ch: 你先原路返回吧，下次再来。
t_en: Retrace your steps for now. We'll try again next time.

t_ch: 集装箱探索真是危险呐。
t_en: These freight containers are just too dangerous.

t_ch: 这里有一处暗门，就是这个！原来这就是正确的道路啊！
t_en: I see a hidden door... Yes, this is it! This is the correct route!

t_ch: 保险箱就在这里！嘿！认路的，你也沿着我刚才的路过来吧！
t_en: Aha, the security safe, there you are! My pathfinding friend, why don't you retrace my steps and join me?

t_ch: 你勉强回忆着刚才解读出的路线，穿过了集装箱组成的巨大迷宫。
t_en: Trying your best to recall the directions you just decoded, you make your way through the gigantic maze of freight containers.

t_ch: 为了找到保险箱中的东西，我可没少花力气，幸好，今天终于算是被我找到了！
t_en: This security safe really made me work for it, but all that effort has paid off. It's finally in my hands!

t_ch: 这都是多亏了你，我的恩人！
t_en: And it's all thanks to you, my friend! I owe you big-time!

t_ch: 用机巧鸟不就直接能飞过来吗？
t_en: Couldn't you have flown here with the cycrane?

t_ch: 那样就不浪漫了！

t_ch: 为什么集装箱里全都是通路呢？
t_en: Why are there so many pathways in the containers?

t_ch: 因为路就在那里。
t_en: Because that's where the path lies.

t_ch: 啊！一种说不清的成就感，油然而生。
t_en: Ah, this sense of achievement welling up within me!

t_ch: 费这么大的劲要找的是什么呢？
t_en: So, what is this thing that you worked so hard to look for?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，是一张存着集装箱内部空间位置的芯片。有了它，我可以将它安装在机巧鸟上，自由获取证物仓库里的赃物了！
t_en: I suppose I can be honest with you: It's a microchip containing navigation data on the spaces within the freight containers. After I install this baby on the cycrane, the spoils in the evidence storage warehouse will all be ripe for the picking!

t_ch: 当然，你放心，我是不会给十王司添麻烦的，我有分寸。
t_en: Before you say anything, don't worry, I know what I can do and what I can't. I won't make any trouble for the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 别的我不问了，报酬快交给我。
t_en: I won't say anymore, then. Now give me my reward!

t_ch: 别急，别急。
t_en: Easy, buddy.

t_ch: 你就像是用绳子牵引着我，让我在漆黑的箱子与箱子之间来回穿梭，实在是太可靠了！
t_en: I must say, the way you guided me from crate to crate in the dark, it's like you were leading me around with a rope. I've never known a more dependable ally!

t_ch: 这是您应得的报酬，这只小鸟也该回到囚室里了，有缘再见！
t_en: Here, you earned this. Well, it's about time for this birdie to return to his cell. May we meet again!

t_ch: 您原路返回就可以回去了！
t_en: To return from where you came, just double back!

t_ch: 这里好像什么都没有，不太对呀，会不会是密文的解读有问题？
t_en: Something's not right. There doesn't seem to be anything here... Is there a mistake with the decryption?

t_ch: 集装箱探索，结束！白鸟又从集装箱中回到了地面。
t_en: Freight container exploration, over! Shiratori exits the container and lands.

t_ch: …我想起来了，「W」为「向上」，「S」为「向下」，「D」为「向右」，「A」为往左。其他的字母无关紧要，只用分辨它们的顺序就可以了！
t_en: ...I remember now. "W" corresponds to "up," "S" to "down," "D" to "right," and "A" to "left." You only need to arrange them in the right order. The rest of the letters don't matter!

t_ch: 我们再试一遍吧。
t_en: Let's try this again.

t_ch: 嘿，别出声，往这边来一点。
t_en: Shh, keep it down. Now come closer.

t_ch: 就在这里，你确定周围没有狱卒对吧？来这里。
t_en: Over here! You're sure there aren't any prison guards nearby? Come on, this way.

t_ch: （白鸟要的纸条应该就在这附近……）
t_en: (The memo that Shiratori is looking for should be somewhere around here...)

t_ch: 你常常在大型场馆、或者会议入口，或者交通枢纽，见过这样的行李安检的传输带。为了公众的安全考虑，仔细查验包裹中是否携带违禁设备。
t_en: You often see conveyor belts like this used for baggage screening at large-scale venues, conference hall entrances, and transport hubs. Out of concern for public safety, packages are carefully screened for prohibited items.

t_ch: 安检机中存在射线，说得直白一些，安检机器中是有辐射的。很多人害怕机器内有辐射存在，似乎在旁边多待一秒就会致癌。实际上，辐射确实是存在的，但没有这么危险。
t_en: This security scanner uses X-rays. Put simply, the scanner emits radiation. Many are afraid of the radiation and fear that staying next to the machine for even a second longer will cause them to develop cancer. The truth is that while it does emit radiation, it doesn't pose a health risk.

t_ch: 若是以「骨折了，要去医院拍个片」的辐射量（不做防护措施）作比较的话，安检机中大概只有那辐射量的十分之一。所以，以后有人用安检机吓唬你时，你可以堂堂正正地反驳！
t_en: When compared to an X-ray examination at the hospital, the amount of radiation a security scanner exposes you to is only one-tenth of what you'd receive from an X-ray scan for a bone fracture (without taking precautions). So, if anyone ever tries to scare you with security scanners and the "health risks" they pose, you can now refute them with confidence!

t_ch: 当然，竞锋舰的安保人员不推荐任何人、以任何理由，登上传送带。
t_en: That said, the Skysplitter security team does not recommend climbing onto the conveyor belt under any circumstances, regardless of who you are.

t_ch: （观察一下四周）
t_en: (Observe the surroundings)

t_ch: 好像并没有人看着你，包括*工作人员*。
t_en: No one, not even the staff, is looking at you.

t_ch: 我好像没有什么行李？
t_en: I don't seem to have a lot of luggage.

t_ch: 确实。虽然说你捡到了很多宝贝，但是它们不太占地方，随取随用。
t_en: That's true. Although you've come to own many trinkets, they don't take up much space and are always within easy reach.

t_ch: 虽然你可能不在意，但是不妨告诉你吧，周围好像没有人盯着你。
t_en: You might not care, but I'll tell you this anyway: No one seems to be looking at you.

t_ch: 谢谢你的小知识，我要走了。
t_en: Thanks for the bit of trivia. I have to go.

t_ch: 当然，不客气。
t_en: Of course. You're welcome.

t_ch: 嗯哼…再声明一次，登上传送带是一件不安全、也不符合法律法规的事情。竞锋舰的安保人员不推荐任何人、以任何理由，登上传送带。
t_en: Ahem. I repeat: Climbing onto the conveyor belt can result in bodily harm and is against the law. The Skysplitter security team does not recommend climbing onto the conveyor belt under any circumstances, regardless of who you are.

t_ch: 我偏要登上去试试！
t_en: I'm doing it anyway!

t_ch: 果然没有看错你！
t_en: I knew I was right about you!

t_ch: 谢谢你的小知识，我要走了。
t_en: Thanks for the bit of trivia. I have to go.

t_ch: 当然，不客气。
t_en: Of course. You're welcome.

t_ch: 不过，进入安检机器后你需要扮演好*一件行李*，你明白吗？一件*真正的行李*。行李是不会说话，也不会乱动的，只有经历考验的行李才能通过传送带，能做到吗？
t_en: You must play the part of luggage once you're inside the security scanner. A real piece of luggage. Remember, luggage does not speak or move, and only luggage that pass muster can make it across the conveyor belt. Can you do it?

t_ch: 能！
t_en: Yes, I can!

t_ch: 光是口头上说说可不算。你需要用实际行动证明自己。
t_en: Action speaks louder than words. You have to walk the talk.

t_ch: 不能！
t_en: No, I can't.

t_ch: 那么你不应该通过！
t_en: Then you shall not pass!

t_ch: 记住，你现在是*一件行李*。
t_en: Remember, you're now a piece of luggage.

t_ch: 这附近应该有一个纸板箱，带着它过来，你就能成为*一件行李*。
t_en: There should be a cardboard box nearby. Bring it over, and you'll be well on your way to becoming a piece of luggage.

t_ch: 在你眼前的毫无疑问是一个「空箱子」，它是如此空空如也，刚好能放下一人。
t_en: There's no doubt that the object before you is an empty box. It even has just enough space to fit a person.

t_ch: 进入传送带的只能是行李？哼，所谓答案啊，大概只是形式上的常识罢了。
t_en: Only luggage is allowed on the conveyor belt? Hmph. That's just what they would have you believe.

t_ch: 我将以箱子的形态出击！
t_en: Box form, engage!

t_ch: 很好看来你进入状态了！
t_en: That's the spirit!

t_ch: 这简直是科技与智慧的结晶…
t_en: This is the true culmination of knowledge and technology...

t_ch: 这很*盒理*。
t_en: Thinking outside the box, if you will.

t_ch: 这下就天衣无缝了。根据一个经验丰富的潜行战术专家所说，没人会对一个*会动的纸板箱*产生半点怀疑。
t_en: Perfect. According to a certain master spy, nobody will ever give a moving cardboard box a second glance.

t_ch: 带着箱子去传送带的入口吧。
t_en: Now, take the box to the conveyor belt entrance.

t_ch: 看样子，你想登上传输带了。那么希望你能明白，这件事只能在竞锋舰上被允许（工作人员看着的话，也是不允许的），要是你在其他地方这样做了，你会受到惩罚。
t_en: It looks like you'll be climbing onto the conveyor belt soon. Remember, this is only allowed on the Skysplitter (and only when the staff isn't looking). If you try this anywhere else, there will be penalties to face.

t_ch: 只有经历考验的行李才能通过传送带，你必须成为一件*真正的行李*，能做到吗？
t_en: Only luggage that pass muster can make it across the conveyor belt. You must become a real piece of luggage. Can you do it?

t_ch: 能！
t_en: Yes, I can!

t_ch: 那好，传送带的小冒险要开始了！
t_en: Very well. Let the conveyor belt adventure begin!

t_ch: 我要退出！
t_en: No, I quit.

t_ch: 那么你不应该通过。回去吧！
t_en: Then you shall not pass. Farewell.

t_ch: 很快你进入到了安检机器的内部，里面什么也看不见，只有机器运作的声音。
t_en: You soon reach the inside of the security scanner. Unable to see anything, the whirring of running machinery is the only thing you hear.

t_ch: 随后，你听到了扬声器里传来了工作人员的声音。
t_en: A moment later, you hear a staff member's voice coming through the speaker.

t_ch: 什么情况？这也是一件行李吗？
t_en: Huh? Is that supposed to be luggage?

t_ch: 我是一件行李！
t_en: That's right. I'm luggage!

t_ch: 请你出去！
t_en: Please leave the area at once!

t_ch: 我不是一件行李！
t_en: No, I'm not luggage!

t_ch: 请你出去！
t_en: Please leave the area at once!

t_ch: *沉默*
t_en: *keep quiet*

t_ch: 能听见吗？
t_en: Hello? Can you hear me?

t_ch: 虽然说行李确实有各式各样的，如此像人的还是少见…我再问一遍，你是人吗？
t_en: I know luggage comes in all shapes and sizes, but it's not every day you see one that looks so human-like... I repeat: Are you human?

t_ch: 我是一件行李！
t_en: No, I'm luggage!

t_ch: 请你出去！
t_en: Please leave the area at once!

t_ch: 我不是人！
t_en: No, I'm not!

t_ch: 请你出去！
t_en: Please leave the area at once!

t_ch: 你能看见我的星核吗？
t_en: Can you see my Stellaron?

t_ch: 什么星核？请你出去！
t_en: What Stellaron? Please leave the area at once!

t_ch: *沉默*
t_en: *keep quiet*

t_ch: 看来真是一件行李，通…通过吧。
t_en: Huh, it really is a piece of luggage. I suppose it can pass...

t_ch: 只有经历考验的行李才能通过传送带，恭喜你完成了考验。
t_en: Only luggage that pass muster can make it across the conveyor belt. Congratulations, you passed the test.

t_ch: 你没有通过安检机器的考验。
t_en: You failed the security scanner's test.

t_ch: 请不要给我们添乱了！
t_en: Please stop messing around and giving us trouble!

t_ch: 毕竟你没有支付行李费用，你应该是不会被分拣到行李储存区的。
t_en: Since you didn't pay the luggage handling fee, you wouldn't have been sent to the luggage storage area anyway.

t_ch: 至此，你合格地扮演了一件行李，并成功完成了传输带冒险…就这样悄悄离开这里吧，别被认识的人发现了，祝你好运！
t_en: You've successfully played the part of luggage and completed the conveyor belt adventure. It's now time to slip away without drawing attention from anyone you know. Good luck!

t_ch: 这位{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，老夫一眼就看出你骨骼惊奇，气宇不凡。如同天上的救星，人间的太岁，必是位响当当的人物。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, I can see with a sidelong glance that you wield great power, and your aura is like no other. Like a savior from the heavens or a legend among men. I am certain that you must be a figure of great renown!

t_ch: 就好比，好比…好比是那星穹列车的无名客。
t_en: You're almost like, like... Like a Nameless of the Astral Express!

t_ch: 词儿呢？辛辛苦苦排练这么久，忘了？
t_en: Get with the script! We spent all that time rehearsing, and you've forgotten already?

t_ch: 你写的那些台词太过于羞耻了，我光看着就浑身起鸡皮疙瘩，实在是拉不下这张老脸。
t_en: That script you wrote was way too embarrassing. Just reading it made my whole body cringe! I just can't bring myself to say it.

t_ch: 说吧，多半是有事相求吧？
t_en: Go on. I imagine you want help with something?

t_ch: 瞧，人家多聪明，不像某些人说话都磕磕绊绊，平时那股子劲儿去哪儿了？
t_en: See, look how smart they are. They don't trip over their words like someone I know. Where's that usual spirit of yours gone?

t_ch: 求人办事和嗑瓜子聊天，能一样嘛。
t_en: It's not like I can ask someone for help like I'm kicking back and catching up with a friend, is it?

t_ch: 首先声明，我不帮人跑腿！
t_en: Before you start, I don't run errands.

t_ch: 老白你看看，你看你把人孩子吓得！咱们一句话还没提，人家就把咱们嘴堵上了。
t_en: Look what you've done, Old Bai. You've got them all shaken up! We've not even said anything, and they've shut us down.

t_ch: 年轻人和你开玩笑呢，这么死板干什么。
t_en: They're just joking. Stop being so serious!

t_ch: （预感到会有麻烦，扭头就走）
t_en: (I sense this is going to be a pain. Time to turn and leave.)

t_ch: 你下意识明白这俩人并无恶意，只是他们求你办的事需要些时间。
t_en: You subconsciously realize that while these two mean you no harm, what they want from you will take up a lot of time.

t_ch: 你面上挂着「如果有时间我会回来听你们说什么」的微笑，倒退，倒退，再倒退到了对话之外的安全区。
t_en: You give them a smile that says: "If I get time, I'll come back and hear you out," then back away, back away and keep backing away until you reach a safe zone outside of this conversation.

t_ch: 不过实不相瞒，我们确实有事相求，我听说星穹列车无名客总是乐于助人。
t_en: I shall not deceive you. We do indeed have something we'd like to ask of you. I hear the Nameless of the Astral Express are always happy to help others?

t_ch: 难道不是你认为无名客干坏事都很难被发现吗？或者是被发现了，上面也不责怪吗？
t_en: I thought you said if the Nameless were up to no good, they'd never be found out. Or even if they were, the higher-ups would never hold them responsible?

t_ch: 不是！你咋老揭咱自己的短？
t_en: No! Why do you keep trying to make us look bad?

t_ch: 你俩要不先统一下意见？
t_en: Why don't I give you a moment to get on the same page?

t_ch: 哥你先合上嘴…好了，这样我们就统一意见了。
t_en: Brother, shut your mouth for just a second... Alright, now we're on the same page.

t_ch: 说到底还是做坏事是吧？
t_en: Let's be honest. You're up to no good, aren't you?

t_ch: 别听他胡说，这是好事！
t_en: Don't listen to him. We're do-gooders!

t_ch: 你们打算说多久，我找把椅子。
t_en: How long are you planning on talking? I'll grab a chair.

t_ch: 没了没了，就这几句。
t_en: Alright, I'll make it quick.

t_ch: 咳咳，先容许我先介绍一下。这位仁科老师，在退役前是带着星槎方阵接受过元帅检阅的云骑教习，虽然现在只是个话痨的机巧鸟控。
t_en: Ahem, first, please allow us to introduce ourselves. This gentleman here is Master Renke. Before he retired, he was a Cloud Knight drillmaster who led a starskiff formation to undergo marshal inspection. Although now, he is no more than a babbling cycrane operator.

t_ch: 而我，一位睿智而低调的工造司工程师，你脚下的这艘竞锋舰，其中火力系统的组件就是我参与设计的，只不过嘛，我现在就跟这艘大船一样，退休了。
t_en: As for myself, I am a wise and low-profile Artisanship Commission engineer. I actually collaborated on the design of a number of parts inside the firepower systems of the Skysplitter you stand upon now. However, I am now also retired, like the ship itself.

t_ch: 自打退役以后，它成了赛事场馆，在新的使命中焕发生机。作为老父亲，我自然是很欣慰的，但内心也是很矛盾的——我那些「多就是好，大就是美」的舰炮怎么办呢？
t_en: Ever since its retirement, it has become a venue for all sorts of competitions and flourished in its new role. Seeing myself as a father to this ship, I admire it greatly, but I am also conflicted... What is to be done about those cannons? Did we not say: "The more the better, the bigger the more beautiful?"

t_ch: 只能放在那里落灰。
t_en: They are merely left there to gather dust.

t_ch: 这么多炮台留着不用，简直是暴殄天物！就算不放实弹，放点礼炮听个响也行啊！
t_en: Leaving so many cannons unused is just a waste of resources! Even if you don't use live ammunition, just firing off a salute and hearing them roar would do!

t_ch: 嘿，你猜怎么着，人家不让。
t_en: Heh, but guess what? They wouldn't let us.

t_ch: 所以，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，我想请你帮我个小忙。
t_en: So, {F#miss}{M#mister}, we wanted you to help us with a little favor.

t_ch: 我批准，您可以在竞锋舰放炮！
t_en: I give you permission. You are allowed to fire your cannons on the Skysplitter!

t_ch: 你批准了也不算数啊，我呢，是想让你去放炮！
t_en: Your permission doesn't mean anything. What I'm asking, is that you go and fire the cannons for us!

t_ch: 你不会打算让我放点炮听个响？
t_en: You're not asking me to go and fire these cannons just so you can listen to them?

t_ch: 不愧是年轻人，我正有此意。
t_en: I knew you'd get it, youngster! That's exactly what I hoped.

t_ch: 我们先听听仁科老师什么需求…
t_en: I'd like to hear what Master Renke wants from me, first...

t_ch: 不行，需求也分先来后到的，你得先和我对需求。
t_en: No! First come, first served. You have to do what I want first.

t_ch: 我早就想开炮了！
t_en: I've always wanted to fire those cannons!

t_ch: 仁科你瞧，就说这小家伙是咱的救星！
t_en: See, Renke? I told you they were our savior!

t_ch: 我看这{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}确实能处。
t_en: I reckon {F#miss}{M#mister} could actually handle it!

t_ch: 不会出什么事吧？
t_en: This won't cause any trouble, will it?

t_ch: 这能出什么事呢？
t_en: What trouble could it cause?

t_ch: 不会出事的话，你自己为什么不上呢？
t_en: If it isn't going to cause trouble, why don't you do it yourself?

t_ch: 你拿好，这枚玉兆里有我已经写好的程序。只要你将它从接入竞锋舰系统，这里面程序就会立刻接管舰炮系统，按照特定顺序对空连射二十一发，炸出个虎虎生风。
t_en: Take this. I've written a program into this jade abacus. You just need to insert it into the Skysplitter system and the program will immediately take control of the cannon systems and set off a <unbreak>21</unbreak>-gun salute in a set order. It'll be a real blast!

t_ch: 鉴于这充满艺术性的表现，我将这个程序命名为——演武仪典二十一响！
t_en: Given the artistic nature of this display, I've named this program... the "Wardance <unbreak>21</unbreak>-Gun Salute"!

t_ch: 等你看到那锣鼓喧天、舰炮齐鸣、旌旗招展、人山人海的场面，就能理解什么叫做「礼炮精神」了！这炮，你一定要放，非放不可，不放就后悔去吧！
t_en: Once you see them lighting up the skies, hear the cannons roaring and catch sight of the waves of people under the waving flags, you will understand what "cannon spirit" means! You have to set them off. You must! You'll regret it if you don't!

t_ch: 嘁…礼炮神经。
t_en: Pfft... cannon nerd.

t_ch: 不会炸到人吗？
t_en: No one's going to get blown up, are they?

t_ch: 放心，我这「演武仪典二十一响」最厉害的就在于，没炮它也照样能响！
t_en: Relax, the amazing thing about this "Wardance <unbreak>21</unbreak>-Gun Salute" is that they can still roar out without even being loaded!

t_ch: 你只管狠狠地发射，办法由老白来想。
t_en: You just focus on firing those cannons and let me take care of the rest.

t_ch: 别人会让我放炮？
t_en: They're just going to let me fire them?

t_ch: 只要你装出一幅自己是来上班的样子，控制室里的人都会选择性无视你。
t_en: As long as you pretend you're working, the people in the control room will selectively ignore you.

t_ch: 如果还有人要来拦你，你就告诉他们，你是来修空调的。
t_en: If anyone tries to stop you, just tell them that you're there to fix the air conditioning.

t_ch: 万一出事怎么办？
t_en: What if something goes wrong?

t_ch: 不成功便成仁，这炮，咱是非放不可的。
t_en: You have to make it work. We'll fire those cannons at any cost.

t_ch: 我绝不会害你的，若放完这炮之后惹出祸端，你就说「那都是老白非要我放的，非要怪就怪老白吧！」他们一定会叹口气，然后放了你的。
t_en: I won't cause you trouble. If things get sticky after you fire the cannons, just tell them, "Old Bai made me do it. If you must blame someone, blame him!" They will just sigh and let you go.

t_ch: 你就把咱的名字供出来，让他们来抓我就好。
t_en: Just give them our names and tell them to come and get us.

t_ch: 这玉兆，就交到你的手里了。请务必，千万，一定要把它插进控制室的系统里。
t_en: I'll hand this jade abacus over to you. Please, you absolutely, definitely, undeniably must insert it into the control room system.

t_ch: 至于我的事，等你凯旋再说吧。
t_en: As for what I want, we can discuss that on your triumphant return.

t_ch: 你可算回来了，我、我，其实在找你……
t_en: You're finally back. I... I've been looking for you...

t_ch: 请务必，千万，一定要把玉兆插进控制室的控制台，一定！
t_en: Please, you absolutely, definitely, undeniably must insert it into the control room system.

t_ch: 至于我的事，等你凯旋再说吧。
t_en: As for what I want, we can discuss that on your triumphant return.

t_ch: 已经好久都没见老白来闹腾了，他不会真的安静了吧？
t_en: It's been a long time since Old Bai has come stirring up trouble. You don't think he's really given up, do you?

t_ch: 老白这人爱舰如命，竞锋舰既是他亲手设计的孩子，也是仙舟的脸面。
t_en: Old Bai treasures this ship like he does his own life. The Skysplitter is his child that he designed himself, and it is the face of the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 孩子光荣退役了，当父亲的想再看看它鸣响炮的光辉时刻，也不是不能理解。
t_en: His child has made an honorable retirement. As its father, you can understand why he would want to see it shining as it fires its cannons once more.

t_ch: 屁！要我说，那老头子就是爱看放炮的大场面——
t_en: Nonsense! If you ask me, that old geezer just likes watching explosions...

t_ch: 喂——！这位朋友，这里可不是你该随便来的地方啊。
t_en: Heyyyy! My friend, this is not a place for you to be wandering around.

t_ch: （无视他们）
t_en: (Ignore him)

t_ch: 嘿嘿，说你呢——
t_en: Hey, I'm talking to you...

t_ch: （你知道这人啥来头吗？）
t_en: (Do you know who that is?)

t_ch: （没见过啊，不会是啥领导吧……）
t_en: (I've never seen {M#him}{F#her} before. {M#He's}{F#She's} not some kind of leadership, is {M#he}{F#she}...)

t_ch: （哪个领导啊？）
t_en: (Which leader?)

t_ch: 趁着二人疑惑之际，你不疾不徐走到控制台前。
t_en: As they descend into confusion, you unhurriedly approach the control station.

t_ch: 我是来修空调的。
t_en: I'm here to fix the AC.

t_ch: 哦！原来是维修——所以，我们空调坏了？
t_en: Oh! You're here to fix... Wait, is our AC broken?

t_ch: 坏了吗？好像是坏了吧，我不确定，你让人家查查。
t_en: Is it? I think it might be. I'm not sure. Get someone to check.

t_ch: 在二人的注视下，你不慌不忙地掏出玉兆，假做出维修的姿态，慢慢走近控制台。
t_en: Under the gaze of the two operators, you casually pull out the jade abacus and pretend to be fixing it as you slowly approach the control station.

t_ch: 别开枪，是老白让我来的。
t_en: Don't shoot! Old Bai told me to come!

t_ch: 你看我说啥了，我倒要看看，老白还能整出什么活来。
t_en: What did I tell you! I actually want to see what Old Bai is playing at this time.

t_ch: 他要放炮。
t_en: He wants me to fire the cannons.

t_ch: 你让他自己来。
t_en: Tell him to come himself.

t_ch: 他说「我爱你」。
t_en: He wants me to tell you: "I love you."
